heading: Estuary habitat restoration program
label: 2903 Estuary habitat restoration program

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: There is established an estuary habitat restoration program under which the Secretary may carry out estuary habitat restoration projects and provide technical assistance through the award of contracts and cooperative agreements in accordance with the requirements of this chapter.

children.heading: Origin of projects
children.label: b Origin of projects
children.text: A proposed estuary habitat restoration project shall originate from a non-Federal interest consistent with State or local laws.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall select estuary habitat restoration projects from a list of project proposals submitted by the Estuary Habitat Restoration Council under .section 2904(b) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: address restoration needs identified in an estuary habitat restoration plan;

children.label: B
children.text: be consistent with the estuary habitat restoration strategy developed under ;section 2905 of this title

children.label: C
children.text: include a monitoring plan that is consistent with standards for monitoring developed under to ensure that short-term and long-term restoration goals are achieved; andsection 2906 of this title

children.label: D
children.text: include satisfactory assurance from the non-Federal interests proposing the project that the non-Federal interests will have adequate personnel, funding, and authority to carry out items of local cooperation and properly maintain the project.

children.label: A
children.text: Whether the project is part of an approved Federal or State estuary management or habitat restoration plan.

children.label: B
children.text: The technical feasibility of the project.

children.label: C
children.text: The scientific merit of the project.

children.label: D
children.text: Whether the project will encourage increased coordination and cooperation among Federal, State, and local government agencies.

children.label: E
children.text: Whether the project fosters public-private partnerships and uses Federal resources to encourage increased private sector involvement, including consideration of the amount of private funds or in-kind contributions for an estuary habitat restoration activity.

children.label: F
children.text: Whether the project is cost-effective.

children.label: G
children.text: Whether the State in which the non-Federal interest is proposing the project has a dedicated source of funding to acquire or restore estuary habitat, natural areas, and open spaces for the benefit of estuary habitat restoration or protection.

children.label: H
children.text: Other factors that the Secretary determines to be reasonable and necessary for consideration.

children.label: A
children.text: the project occurs within a watershed in which there is a program being carried out that addresses sources of pollution and other activities that otherwise would re-impair the restored habitat; or

children.label: B
children.text: the project includes pilot testing of or a demonstration of an innovative technology or approach having the potential for improved cost-effectiveness in estuary habitat restoration.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2) and subsection (e)(2), the Federal share of the cost of an estuary habitat restoration project (other than the cost of operation and maintenance of the project) carried out under this chapter shall not exceed 65 percent of such cost.

children.heading: Costs
children.label: i Costs
children.text: The costs of monitoring an estuary habitat restoration project funded under this chapter may be included in the total cost of the estuary habitat restoration project.

children.label: I
children.text: to measure the effectiveness of the restoration project; and

children.label: II
children.text: to allow adaptive management to ensure project success.

children.heading: Innovative technology costs
children.label: 2 Innovative technology costs
children.text: The Federal share of the incremental additional cost of including in a project pilot testing of or a demonstration of an innovative technology or approach described in subsection (c)(4)(B) shall be 85 percent.

children.heading: Non-Federal share
children.label: 3 Non-Federal share
children.text: The non-Federal share of the cost of an estuary habitat restoration project carried out under this chapter shall include lands, easements, rights-of-way, and relocations and may include services (including monitoring), or any other form of in-kind contribution determined by the Secretary to be an appropriate contribution equivalent to the monetary amount required for the non-Federal share of the activity.

children.heading: Operation and maintenance
children.label: 4 Operation and maintenance
children.text: The non-Federal interests shall be responsible for all costs associated with operating, maintaining, replacing, repairing, and rehabilitating all projects carried out under this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Pending completion of the estuary habitat restoration strategy to be developed under , the Secretary may take interim actions to carry out an estuary habitat restoration activity.section 2905 of this title

children.heading: Federal share
children.label: 2 Federal share
children.text: The Federal share of the cost of an estuary habitat restoration activity before the completion of the estuary habitat restoration strategy shall not exceed 25 percent of such cost.

children.label: A
children.text: provide all lands, easements, rights-of-way, and relocations and any other elements the Secretary determines appropriate under subsection (d)(3); and

children.label: B
children.text: provide for long-term maintenance and monitoring of the project.

children.heading: Nongovernmental organizations
children.label: 2 Nongovernmental organizations
children.text: Notwithstanding , for any project to be undertaken under this chapter, the Secretary, in consultation and coordination with appropriate State and local governmental agencies and Indian tribes, may allow a nongovernmental organization to serve as the non-Federal interest for the project.section 1962d–5b(b) of title 42

children.heading: Project agreements
children.label: 3 Project agreements
children.text: For a project carried out under this chapter, the requirements of may be fulfilled by a nongovernmental organization serving as the non-Federal interest for the project pursuant to paragraph (2).section 2213(j)(1) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In carrying out this chapter, the Secretary may delegate project implementation to another Federal department or agency on a reimbursable basis if the Secretary, upon the recommendation of the Council, determines such delegation is appropriate.

children.heading: Small project defined
children.label: A Small project defined
children.text: In this paragraph, the term “small project” means a project carried out under this chapter with an estimated Federal cost of less than $1,000,000.

children.label: i
children.text: the Secretary of the Interior (acting through the Director of the United States Fish and Wildlife Service);

children.label: ii
children.text: the Under Secretary for Oceans and Atmosphere of the Department of Commerce;

children.label: iii
children.text: the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency; or

children.label: iv
children.text: the Secretary of Agriculture.

children.heading: Funding
children.label: C Funding
children.text: A small project delegated to the head of a Federal department or agency under this paragraph may be carried out using funds appropriated to the department or agency under or other funds available to the department or agency.section 2908(a)(1) of this title

children.heading: Agreements
children.label: D Agreements
children.text: The head of a Federal department or agency to which a small project is delegated under this paragraph shall enter into an agreement with the non-Federal interest for the project generally in conformance with the criteria in subsections (d) and (e). Cooperative agreements may be used for any delegated project to allow the non-Federal interest to carry out the project on behalf of the Federal agency.
